title: Cylindropuntia × tetracantha
taxonomy: Cylindropuntia × tetracantha is a hybrid species of the genus Cylindropuntia, which is a group of cacti. The Cylindropuntia genus is classified under the family Cactaceae. Within this family, the Cylindropuntia genus is a subset of the tribe Opuntieae. The classification of Cylindropuntia × tetracantha is further specified as a hybrid of Cylindropuntia species, with its exact classification depending on the specific parent species involved. As a result, the detailed classification of Cylindropuntia × tetracantha can vary depending on the source and the specific parent species.

intro: Cylindropuntia × tetracantha, also known as a hybrid of the cholla cactus, is a perennial plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its cylindrical stems and sharp, barbed spines. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for wound healing, digestive issues, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Cylindropuntia × tetracantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Cylindropuntia × tetracantha include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, infections, and gastrointestinal disorders.
